description: Native Swift iOS App — Phase 3: Shopping List Items (add, view, edit, check, delete, undo, realtime sync, store+category grouping)

userStories.title: Item Codable Model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Swift model for shopping list items that maps to the Supabase items table so I can decode/encode item data.
acceptanceCriteria: Item struct conforms to Codable and Identifiable
Properties: id (UUID), listId (UUID), name (String), category (String?), isChecked (Bool), storeId (UUID?), quantity (Int), price (Decimal?), imageUrl (String?), addedAt (Date)
CodingKeys map to snake_case column names (list_id, is_checked, store_id, image_url, added_at)
Default values: quantity = 1, isChecked = false, price = nil, imageUrl = nil, category = nil, storeId = nil
HistoryEntry struct: id (UUID), userId (UUID), name (String), addedAt (Date) with matching CodingKeys

Store struct: id (UUID), userId (UUID), name (String), color (String?), categories (jsonb decoded as [CategoryDef]), sortOrder (Int), createdAt (Date) with CodingKeys mapping (user_id, sort_order, created_at)
CategoryDef struct: key (String), name (String), color (String), keywords ([String])
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Item, HistoryEntry, Store, and CategoryDef Codable models with CodingKeys for snake_case mapping. Build succeeds.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Pure data models with no logic

userStories.title: Category Definitions & Auto-Categorization
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the 12 default category definitions and the auto-categorization algorithm in Swift so new items can be automatically categorized.
acceptanceCriteria: CategoryDefinitions enum/struct contains all 12 default categories with keys, display names, colors, and keyword arrays matching the React implementation exactly
categorizeItem(_ name: String, categories: [CategoryDef]?) function implements 3-pass matching: exact → multi-word phrase → single-word fallback → other
When categories parameter is nil or empty, uses the 12 defaults
When categories are provided (from a store), uses those instead
Case-insensitive matching
categorizeItem("Banana") returns "produce"
categorizeItem("Frozen Pizza") returns "frozen" (multi-word match)
categorizeItem("XYZ Widget") returns "other" (no match)
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: CategoryDefinitions enum with all 12 default categories matching React exactly. 3-pass categorizeItem function (exact, multi-word phrase, single-word fallback). category(forKey:) helper. Build succeeds.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Algorithm with 3-pass matching needs correctness validation

userStories.title: ItemService — CRUD Operations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service layer for item CRUD so view models can perform all item operations through a clean API.
acceptanceCriteria: ItemService class with methods: fetchItems(listId:), addItem(listId:, name:, category:, storeId:), updateItem(itemId:, updates:), toggleItem(itemId:, isChecked:), deleteItem(itemId:), deleteItems(itemIds:), restoreItem(listId:, itemData:)
addItem capitalizes the first letter of the name (matching React behavior)
addItem auto-categorizes using categorizeItem() with the store's categories if storeId is provided
All methods use SupabaseManager.shared.client
All methods throw descriptive errors on failure
Does NOT manually update item_count (triggers handle it)
Build succeeds
userStories.testIntensityReason: Service layer with all Supabase CRUD queries

userStories.title: HistoryService — Autocomplete Data
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service to fetch and write item history so the add-item form can offer autocomplete suggestions.
acceptanceCriteria: HistoryService class with methods: fetchHistory(userId:), addHistoryEntry(userId:, name:), addHistoryEntries(userId:, names:)
subscribeHistory(userId:) provides realtime subscription (Postgres changes on history table, filtered by user_id), triggers re-fetch on any change
Deduplicated unique names computed from history (case-insensitive, sorted alphabetically)
Build succeeds
userStories.testIntensityReason: Simple fetch and subscribe with minimal logic

userStories.title: StoreService — Read-Only for Grouping
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to fetch the user's stores so items can be grouped by store in the list view. Full store CRUD is Phase 4.
acceptanceCriteria: StoreService class with methods: fetchStores(userId:), subscribeStores(userId:) with realtime subscription on stores table filtered by user_id
Store categories jsonb decoded into [CategoryDef] array
If a store has an empty categories array, treat it as use defaults
Build succeeds

userStories.title: ListDetailViewModel — @Observable State + Realtime
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a view model that manages the item list state for a single list, including realtime updates, store data, and history.
acceptanceCriteria: ListDetailViewModel is @Observable
Published properties: items: [Item], stores: [Store], history: [HistoryEntry], isLoading: Bool, error: String?

Computed properties: uncheckedItems, checkedItems, groupedByStore (groups unchecked items by storeId with Unassigned group, ordered by sort_order), uniqueHistoryNames (deduplicated, alphabetically sorted)
groupByCategory(_ items: [Item], store: Store?) method groups items by category using store's custom categories or defaults, uncategorized items go into Other, category order follows array order
On init with listId and userId: fetches items, stores, history and sets up realtime subscriptions for all three
Realtime: on any Postgres change to items (filtered by list_id), re-fetches all items
Realtime: on any Postgres change to stores (filtered by user_id), re-fetches all stores
Realtime: on any Postgres change to history (filtered by user_id), re-fetches all history
Action methods: addItem(name:, storeId:), toggleItem(_ item:), deleteItem(_ item:), updateItem(_ itemId:, updates:), clearChecked(), restoreItem(listId:, itemData:)
Cleans up all realtime subscriptions on deinit
Build succeeds
userStories.testIntensityReason: Complex state management with grouping logic and realtime wiring

userStories.title: Add Item Bar — Bottom Input with Autocomplete
userStories.description: As a user, I want a text field at the bottom of the list to quickly add items, with suggestions from my past items, so adding groceries is fast.
acceptanceCriteria: Sticky input bar pinned to the bottom of the screen (above safe area / home indicator)
Text field with placeholder Add an item...
As user types, .searchSuggestions modifier shows matching items from history (case-insensitive substring match)
Tapping a suggestion fills the text field with that name
Add button (or return key) submits: creates item, adds history entry, clears text field
If stores exist, show a store picker (compact menu or segmented control) next to the input so user can optionally assign a store on add
New item auto-categorized based on name + selected store's categories
Item name capitalized (first letter uppercase)
Empty input: Add button disabled, return key does nothing
Input field remains focused after adding (for rapid entry of multiple items)
Build succeeds
Verify on device/simulator

userStories.title: Item List View — Store + Category Grouping
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my items organized by store and then by category so I can shop efficiently aisle by aisle.
acceptanceCriteria: List view shows unchecked items grouped by store (if stores exist), then by category within each store
Each store section has a header showing store name with colored dot, item count, and collapse/expand chevron
Tapping a store header collapses/expands that section
Items without a store appear in an Unassigned section (or directly by category if no stores exist)
Within each store (or flat list), items grouped by category with category header: colored dot, category name, item count
Each item row shows: item name, quantity badge (if qty > 1), store badge (colored pill), category badge (colored pill), price (if set, formatted as $X.XX)
Double-tap on an item row toggles checked/unchecked
Checked items appear in a separate Checked (N) section at the bottom
Clear checked button in checked section header — tap shows confirmation alert, then deletes all checked items
Empty state: Your list is empty with hint Add items below
Pull-to-refresh triggers a full re-fetch of items
Build succeeds
Verify on device/simulator
userStories.testIntensityReason: Complex grouped display with collapsible sections

userStories.title: Item Context Menu — Long-Press Edit
userStories.description: As a user, I want to long-press an item to edit its details so I can change the name, quantity, price, store, or category without leaving the list.
acceptanceCriteria: Long-press on any item row shows a native iOS context menu
Edit Name: Opens an alert with text field pre-filled with current name, save updates item name
Quantity: Shows stepper-style options or submenu with 1-10 + Custom option
Price: Opens an alert with numeric text field, pre-filled with current price (or empty), save updates price, Remove Price option if price is set
Store: Submenu listing all stores + No store, selecting updates storeId, current store shown with checkmark
Category: Submenu listing all categories (from assigned store or defaults) + No category, current category shown with checkmark
Delete: Red destructive button, deletes item immediately (undo via shake covers it)
All changes persist immediately via ItemService.updateItem
When store changes, item keeps its existing category (matches React behavior)
Build succeeds
Verify on device/simulator
userStories.testIntensityReason: Multiple edit actions via native context menus

userStories.title: Swipe-to-Delete with .swipeActions
userStories.description: As a user, I want to swipe left on an item to quickly delete it, using the standard iOS gesture.
acceptanceCriteria: Swipe left on any item row reveals a red Delete action (using .swipeActions(edge: .trailing))
Tapping the delete action removes the item from the list
Deletion pushes an undo action to UndoManager (restored in US-011)
Row animates out on delete (SwiftUI default List animation)
Swipe works on both unchecked and checked items
Build succeeds
Verify on device/simulator

userStories.title: Shake-to-Undo via UndoManager
userStories.description: As a user, I want to shake my phone to undo the last delete so I can recover items I removed by accident.
acceptanceCriteria: When an item is deleted (swipe or context menu), register an undo action with the view's UndoManager
When checked items are cleared, register a single undo action that restores all cleared items
Shaking the device triggers the iOS system Undo alert (Undo Delete Item?)
Confirming undo calls ItemService.restoreItem (or restoreItems for batch) to re-insert items with original data
Restored items appear back in the list (realtime subscription picks them up)
Undo stack depth: at least the last action (iOS UndoManager supports multi-level undo natively)
If no undo actions are available, shake does nothing (no empty alert)
Build succeeds
Verify on device/simulator
userStories.testIntensityReason: UndoManager wiring with restore logic and batch undo

userStories.title: Realtime Sync — Cross-Device Verification
userStories.description: As a user, I want changes made on the web app (or another device) to appear on my phone in real-time so my list is always current.
acceptanceCriteria: Adding an item on the React PWA appears on the Swift app within ~2 seconds (without manual refresh)
Checking/unchecking an item on one client reflects on the other
Deleting an item on one client removes it on the other
Editing item details (name, qty, price, store, category) on one client updates the other
Creating a new store on the React PWA updates the store grouping on the Swift app
Pull-to-refresh works as a manual fallback
Realtime subscription reconnects after app returns from background
Build succeeds
Verify on device/simulator with two clients
